D7 Print L4 Analyze Reference

AM vs FM Screening

two ways to fake grey with one ink: grow the dots on a fixed grid (AM), or scatter equal micro-dots more densely (FM)

Amplitude-modulated (AM) screening keeps dots on a regular grid and varies their size with tone. Frequency-modulated (FM, or stochastic) screening keeps every dot the same tiny size and varies how many are placed. AM is stable on press and prone to moiré; FM resolves finer detail and avoids moiré but gains tone more readily.

Switch screening and slide the pitch over the same dark-to-light ramp. Watch AM dots swell toward the shadows on their grid, while FM keeps one dot size and simply crowds them together.
